1.arabic: بِسْمِ ٱللَّهِ ٱلرَّحْمَـٰنِ ٱلرَّحِيمِ إِذَا وَقَعَتِ ٱلْوَاقِعَةُ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: WHEN WHAT IS to happen comes to pass --

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: When the forthcoming event does occur.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: When the Terror descends

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: When there happeneth Event.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: When the Event (i.e. the Day of Resurrection) befalls.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: When the inevitable occurs.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: When the Event will come to pass

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: When the Waqi`ah occurs

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: When the event befalleth -

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: When the Imminent [Hour] befalls

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: When the Event (the resurrection) comes

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: When the Occurrence occurs,

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: When the inevitable event comes,

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: When the great event comes to pass,

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: When the inevitable event takes place,

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: When the Event inevitable cometh to pass,

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Itha waqaAAati alwaqiAAatu

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Which is bound to happen undoubtedly --

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Then none will be able to deny its occurrence.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: (and none denies its descending)

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: There is about its happening no lie.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And there can be no denying of its befalling.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Of its occurrence, there is no denial.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: – and then there will be no one to deny its occurrence –

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: There is not, for its occurrence, Kadhibah.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: There is no denying that it will befall -

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: —there is no denying that it will befall—

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: there is no denying its coming

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: There is, at its occurrence, no denial.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: no soul will deny its coming.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: There is no belying its coming to pass--

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: and there can be no denying its happening,

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Then will no (soul) entertain falsehood concerning its coming.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Laysa liwaqAAatiha kathibatun

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Degrading (some) and exalting (others);

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: The event will be abasing some, and exalting some.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: abasing, exalting,

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Abasing exalting.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: It will bring low (some); (and others) it will exalt;

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Bringing low, raising high.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: (a calamitous Event) that shall turn things upside down:

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Bringing low (some), exalting (others).

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Abasing (some), exalting (others);

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: [it will be] lowering and exalting.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: (it will) abase (some) and exalt (others).

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: It will bring down [some] and raise up [others].

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: It will abase some and exalt others.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Abasing (one party), exalting (the other),

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: some shall be abased and others exalted.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: (Many) will it bring low; (many) will it exalt;

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Khafidatun rafiAAatun

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: When the earth is shaken up convulsively,

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: When the earth will tremble, shivering.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: when the earth shall be rocked

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: This will happen when the earth is shaken, shaken

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: When the earth will be shaken with a terrible shake.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: When the earth is shaken with a shock.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: when the earth will suddenly shake with a terrible shaking,

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: When the earth will be shaken with a terrible shake.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: When the earth is shaken with a shock

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: When the earth is shaken violently,

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: When the earth is shaken

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: When the earth is shaken with convulsion

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: When the earth is violently shaken

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: When the earth shall be shaken with a (severe) shaking,

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: When the earth is shaken violently,

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: When the earth shall be shaken to its depths,

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Itha rujjati alardu rajjan

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: The mountains bruised and crushed,

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And the mountains will be crushed, blown to bits.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: and the mountains crumbled

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And the mountains are crumbled, crumbled.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And the mountains will be powdered to dust.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: And the mountains are crushed and crumbled.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: and the mountains will crumble

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And the mountains will be powdered to dust,

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And the hills are ground to powder

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: and the mountains are shattered into bits

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: and the mountains fragmented

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And the mountains are broken down, crumbling

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: and the mountains crumbled,

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And the mountains shall be made to crumble with (an awful) crumbling,

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: and the mountains are totally shattered and crumble to pieces

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: And the mountains shall be crumbled to atoms,

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Wabussati aljibalu bassan

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Turned to dust, floating in the air,

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: So they will become like fine dust, scattered in a shaft of light.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: and become a dust, scattered,

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: So that they become dust seattered.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: So that they will become floating dust particles.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: And they become scattered dust.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: and will scatter abroad into fine dust.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: So that they will become floating dust particles.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: So that they become a scattered dust,

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: and become scattered dust,

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: becoming scattered dust,

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And become dust dispersing.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: they will become like dust scattered around.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: So that they shall be as scattered dust.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: and become like scattered dust particles,

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Becoming dust scattered abroad,

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Fakanat habaan munbaththan

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: You will become three categories:

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And you will become divided into three categories.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: and you shall be three bands --

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And ye are classes three.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And you (all) will be in three kinds (i.e. separate groups).

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: And you become three classes.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: You shall then become three groups.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And you (all) will be in three groups.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And ye will be three kinds:

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: you will be three groups:

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: you shall be divided into three parties,

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And you become [of] three kinds:

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: On that day, you (mankind) will be divided into three groups:

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And you shall be three sorts.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: [on that Day] you shall be divided into three groups.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: And ye shall be sorted out into three classes.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Wakuntum azwajan thalathatan

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Those of the right hand -- how (happy) will be those of the right hand!

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: So those on the right – how (fortunate) are those on the right!

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Companions of the Right (O Companions of the Right!)

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Those on the right hand; how happy shall those on the right hand be!

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: So those on the Right Hand (i.e. those who will be given their Records in their right hands), Who will be those on the Right Hand? (As a respect for them, because they will enter Paradise).

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Those on the Right—what of those on the Right?

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: The People on the Right: and how fortunate will be the People on the Right!

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: So those on the right -- how (fortunate) will be those on the right!

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: (First) those on the right hand; what of those on the right hand?

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: The People of the Right Hand—and what are the People of the Right Hand?!

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Companions of the Right, what are the Companions of the Right?

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Then the companions of the right - what are the companions of the right?

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: The people of the right hand - those whose books of records will be placed in their right hands. How happy they will be!

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Then (as to) the companions of the right hand; how happy are the companions of the right hand!

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Those on the Right -- how blessed are those on the Right!

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Then (there will be) the Companions of the Right Hand;- What will be the Companions of the Right Hand?

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Faashabu almaymanati ma ashabu almaymanati

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Then those of the left hand -- how (unhappy) will be those of the left hand!

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And those on the left – how (wretched) are those on the left!

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Companions of the Left (O Companions of the Left!)

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And those on the left hand; how miserable shall those on the left hand be!

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And those on the Left Hand (i.e. those who will be given their Record in their left hands), Who will be those on the Left Hand? (As a disgrace for them, because they will enter Hell).

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: And those on the Left—what of those on the Left?

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: And the People on the Left: and how miserable will be the People on the Left!

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And those on the left -- how (unfortunate) will be those on the left!

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And (then) those on the left hand; what of those on the left hand?

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: And the People of the Left Hand—and what are the People of the Left Hand?!

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Companions of the Left, what are the Companions of the Left?

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And the companions of the left - what are the companions of the left?

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: The people of the left hand - those whose books of records will be placed in their left hands. How miserable they will be!

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And (as to) the companions of the left hand; how wretched are the companions of the left hand!

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Those on the Left -- how unlucky are those on the Left!

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: And the Companions of the Left Hand,- what will be the Companions of the Left Hand?

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Waashabu almashamati ma ashabu almashamati

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Then the foremost, how pre-excellent,

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And those who surpassed have indeed excelled.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: and the Outstrippers: the Outstrippers

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And the preceders are the pre-ceders.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And those foremost [(in Islamic Faith of Monotheism and in performing righteous deeds) in the life of this world on the very first call for to embrace Islam,] will be foremost (in Paradise).

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: And the forerunners, the forerunners.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: As for the Foremost, they will be the foremost!

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And those foremost will be foremost.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And the foremost in the race, the foremost in the race:

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: And the Foremost Ones are the foremost ones:

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: And the Outstrippers, the Outstrippers

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And the forerunners, the forerunners -

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: The foremost ones (in faith and virtue) - the foremost ones in receiving their reward.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And the foremost are the foremost,

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: The third to the fore shall be the foremost.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: And those Foremost (in Faith) will be Foremost (in the Hereafter).

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Waalssabiqoona alssabiqoona

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Who will be honoured

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: It is they who are the close ones.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: those are they brought nigh the Throne,

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Those! they shall be the brought nigh,

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: These will be those nearest to Allah.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Those are the nearest.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: They shall be near-stationed (to their Lord),

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: These will be the nearest (to Allah).

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Those are they who will be brought nigh

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: they are the ones brought near [to Allah],

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: those are they brought near (to their Lord)

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Those are the ones brought near [to Allah]

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: (The foremost ones) will be the nearest ones to God

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: These are they who are drawn nigh (to Allah),

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: They shall be the nearest to God.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: These will be those Nearest to Allah:

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Olaika almuqarraboona

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: In gardens of tranquility;

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: They are in Gardens of peace.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: in the Gardens of Delight

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: In Gardens of Delight.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: In the Gardens of delight (Paradise).

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: In the Gardens of Bliss.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: in the Gardens of Bliss.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: In the Gardens of Delight.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: In gardens of delight;

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: [who will reside] in the gardens of bliss.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: in the Gardens of Delight,

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: In the Gardens of Pleasure,

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: in the beautiful Paradise.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: In the gardens of bliss.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: They will dwell in the Gardens of Bliss:

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: In Gardens of Bliss:

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Fee jannati alnnaAAeemi

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: A number of the earlier peoples,

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: A large group from the earlier generations.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: (a throng of the ancients

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: A multitude from the ancients.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: A multitude of those (foremost) will be from the first generations (who embraced Islam).

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: A throng from the ancients.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: A throng of the ancients,

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: A multitude of those will be from the first ones.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: A multitude of those of old

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: A multitude from the former [generations]

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: a host of the ancients

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: A [large] company of the former peoples

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Many of them will be from the ancient people

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: A numerous company from among the first,

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: a large group of the early believers,

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: A number of people from those of old,

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Thullatun mina alawwaleena

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: And a few of later ages,

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And a few from the latter.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: and how few of the later folk)

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And a few from the later generations.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And a few of those (foremost) will be from the later time (generations).

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: And a small band from the latecomers.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: and a few from later times.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And a few of those will be from the later ones.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And a few of those of later time.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: and a few from the latter ones.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: but only a few from the later generations.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And a few of the later peoples,

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: and only a few of them from the later generations.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And a few from among the latter.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: and a lesser number from the later generations.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: And a few from those of later times.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Waqaleelun mina alakhireena

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: On couches wrought of gold,

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: On studded thrones.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: upon close-wrought couches

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: On couches in wrought with gold.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: (They will be) on thrones woven with gold and precious stones,

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: On luxurious furnishings.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: They (will be seated) on gold-encrusted couches,

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: (They will be) on thrones, Mawdunah.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: On lined couches,

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: On brocaded couches

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: on lavish couches

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: On thrones woven [with ornament],

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: They will recline on jewelled couches

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: On thrones decorated,

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Seated on couches wrought in gold and encrusted with precious stones,

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: (They will be) on Thrones encrusted (with gold and precious stones),

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: AAala sururin mawdoonatin

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Reclining face to face.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Reclining on them, facing each other.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: reclining upon them, set face to face,

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Reclining thereon facing each other.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Reclining thereon, face to face.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Reclining on them, facing one another.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: reclining on them, arrayed face to face;

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Reclining thereon, face to face.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Reclining therein face to face.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: reclining on them, face to face.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: they shall recline, facing each other,

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Reclining on them, facing each other.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: facing one another.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Reclining on them, facing one another.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: reclining on them facing each other;

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Reclining on them, facing each other.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Muttakieena AAalayha mutaqabileena

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Youths of never-ending bloom will pass round to them

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Surrounded by immortal boys.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: immortal youths going round about them

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: There shall go round Unto them youths ever-young.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: They will be served by immortal boys,

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Serving them will be immortalized youth.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: immortal youths shall go about them

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Immortal boys will go around them (serving),

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: There wait on them immortal youths

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: They will be waited upon by immortal youths,

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: (and there) shall wait on them immortal youths

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: There will circulate among them young boys made eternal

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Immortal youths will serve them

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Round about them shall go youths never altering in age,

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: (Saying): "We are indeed Mughramun (i.e. ruined or lost the money without any profit, or punished by the loss of all that we spend for cultivation, etc.)! [See Tafsir Al-Qurtubi, Vol. 17, Page 219]

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: “We are penalized.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: “We have been penalised;

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: (Saying:) "We are indeed Mughramun!"

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Lo! we are laden with debt!

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: ‘Indeed we have suffered loss!

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: (Saying:) 'We are laden with debts!

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: [Saying], "Indeed, we are [now] in debt;

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: crying, "We have been left to suffer loss.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Surely we are burdened with debt:

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: "We are ruined,

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: (Saying), "We are indeed left with debts (for nothing):

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Inna lamughramoona

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Indeed, we have been deprived of the fruits of our labour."

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: “In fact, we were unfortunate!”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: nay, we have been robbed!'

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Aye! we are deprived!

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: "Nay, but we are deprived!"

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: No, we are being deprived.”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: nay; we have been undone!”

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: "Nay, but we are deprived!"

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Nay, but we are deprived!

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: No, we are deprived!’

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Rather, we have been prevented'

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Rather, we have been deprived."

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Surely, we have been deprived (of the benefits)".

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Nay! we are deprived.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: nay, we are deprived [altogether]."

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: "Indeed are we shut out (of the fruits of our labour)"

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Bal nahnu mahroomoona

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Consider the water that you drink.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: So what is your opinion regarding the water that you drink?

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Have you considered the water you drink?

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Behold! - the water which ye drink:

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Tell Me! The water that you drink.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Have you seen the water you drink?

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Did you cast a good look at the water that you drink?

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Do you not see the water that you drink.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Have ye observed the water which ye drink?

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Have you considered the water that you drink?

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Have you thought about the water you drink?

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And have you seen the water that you drink?

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Have you seen the water which you drink?

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Have you considered the water which you drink?

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Have you considered the water that you drink?

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: See ye the water which ye drink?

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Afaraaytumu almaa allathee tashraboona

69.arabic: ءَأَنتُمْ أَنزَلْتُمُوهُ مِنَ ٱلْمُزْنِ أَمْ نَحْنُ ٱلْمُنزِلُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Do you send it down from the clouds, or We send it down?

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Is it you who caused it to descend from the cloud, or is it We Who cause it to descend?

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Did you send it down from the clouds, or did We send it?

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Send it down ye from the raincloud, or are We the Sender down?

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Is it you who cause it from the rainclouds to come down, or are We the Causer of it to come down?

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Is it you who sent it down from the clouds, or are We the Sender?

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Is it you who brought it down from the clouds or is it We Who brought it down?

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Is it you who cause it to come down from Al-Muzn, or are We the Cause of it coming down

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Is it ye who shed it from the raincloud, or are We the Shedder?

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Is it you who bring it down from the rain cloud, or is it We who bring [it] down?

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Is it you that send it down from the clouds or We?

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Is it you who brought it down from the clouds, or is it We who bring it down?

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Is it you who sent it down from the clouds or is it We who have sent it down?

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Is it you that send it down from the clouds, or are We the senders?

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Is it you who cause it to descend from the clouds, or do We?

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Do ye bring it down (in rain) from the cloud or do We?

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Aantum anzaltumoohu mina almuzni am nahnu almunziloona

70.arabic: لَوْ نَشَآءُ جَعَلْنَـٰهُ أُجَاجًا فَلَوْلَا تَشْكُرُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: We could make it brackish, if We pleased; so why do you not acknowledge thanks?

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: If We will We can make it bitter, so why do you not give thanks?

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Did We will, We would make it bitter; so why are you not thankful?

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: If We willed, surely We would make it brackish. Wherefore give ye not thanks?

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: If We willed, We verily could make it salt (and undrinkable), why then do you not give thanks (to Allah)?

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: If We will, We can make it salty. Will you not be thankful?

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: If We had so pleased, We could have made it bitter. So why would you not give thanks?

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: If We willed, We verily could make it salty; why then do you not give thanks

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: If We willed We verily could make it bitter. Why then, give ye not thanks?

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: If We wish We can make it bitter. Then why do you not give thanks?

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: If We will, We would make it bitter, why then do you not give thanks?

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: If We willed, We could make it bitter, so why are you not grateful?

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Had We wanted, We could have made it salty. Why then do you not give thanks?

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: If We pleased, We would have made it salty; why do you not then give thanks?

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: If We so pleased, We certainly could make it salty. Why, then, are you not grateful?

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Were it Our Will, We could make it salt (and unpalatable): then why do ye not give thanks?

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Law nashao jaAAalnahu ojajan falawla tashkuroona

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: So what is your opinion regarding the fire which you kindle?

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Have you considered the fire you kindle?

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Behold! - the fire which ye strike out:

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Tell Me! The fire which you kindle,

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Have you seen the fire you kindle?

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Did you consider the fire which you kindle?

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Do you not see the fire which you kindle.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Have ye observed the fire which ye strike out;

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Have you considered the fire that you kindle?

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Have you thought about the fire you kindle?

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And have you seen the fire that you ignite?

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Have you seen the fire which you kindle?

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Have you considered the fire which you strike?

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Have you thought about the fire that you kindle.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: See ye the Fire which ye kindle?

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Afaraaytumu alnnara allatee tooroona

72.arabic: ءَأَنتُمْ أَنشَأْتُمْ شَجَرَتَهَآ أَمْ نَحْنُ ٱلْمُنشِـُٔونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Have you raised its tree, or We have raised it?

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Is it you who grew its tree, or is it We Who create?

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Did you make its timber to grow, or did We make it?

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Produce ye the tree thereof, or are We the Producer?

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Is it you who made the tree thereof to grow, or are We the Grower?

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Is it you who produce its tree, or are We the Producer?

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Did you make its tree grow or was it We Who made it grow?

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Is it you who made the tree thereof to grow, or are We the Grower

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Was it ye who made the tree thereof to grow, or were We the grower?

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Was it you who caused its tree to grow, or were We the grower?

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Is it you that originated its tree, or are We the Originator?

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Is it you who produced its tree, or are We the producer?

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Is it you who have produced its tree or is it We who have produced it?

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Is it you that produce the trees for it, or are We the producers?

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Did you produce the tree that serves as fuel or do We?

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Is it ye who grow the tree which feeds the fire, or do We grow it?

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Aantum anshatum shajarataha am nahnu almunshioona

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: We have made it as a reminder and convenience for the needy.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: We have made it as a reminder of hell and as a utility for travellers in the jungle.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: We Ourselves made it for a reminder, and a boon to the desert-dwellers.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: We! it is We Who made it a reminder and a provision Unto the campers.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: We have made it a Reminder (for the Hell-fire, in the Hereafter); and an article of use for the travellers (and all the others, in this world).

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: We have made it a reminder, and a comfort for the users.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: We made it a reminder and a provision for the needy.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: We have made it a Reminder, and an article of use for the Muqwin.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: We, even We, appointed it a memorial and a comfort for the dwellers in the wilderness.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: It was We who made it a reminder and a boon for the desert-dwellers.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: We have made it a reminder, and a blessing to the traveler.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: We have made it a reminder and provision for the travelers,

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: It is We who have made it as a reminder and a means of comfort for the people.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: We have made it a reminder and an advantage for the wayfarers of the desert.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: We have made it to be a reminder and a benefit for the wayfarers.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: We have made it a memorial (of Our handiwork), and an article of comfort and convenience for the denizens of deserts.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Nahnu jaAAalnaha tathkiratan wamataAAan lilmuqweena

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: So glorify your Lord, the most supreme.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Therefore (O dear Prophet Mohammed – peace and blessings be upon him) proclaim the Purity of the name of your Lord, the Greatest.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Then magnify the Name of thy Lord, the All-mighty.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Wherefore hallow thou the name of thy Lord, the Mighty.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Then glorify with praises the Name of your Lord, the Most Great.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: So glorify the Name of your Great Lord.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Glorify, then, (O Prophet), the name of your Great Lord.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Then glorify with praises the Name of your Lord, the Most Great.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Therefor (O Muhammad), praise the name of thy Lord, the Tremendous.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: So celebrate the Name of your Lord, the All-supreme.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Then, exalt the Name of your Lord, the Great.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: So exalt the name of your Lord, the Most Great.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: (Muhammad), glorify your Lord, the Great One.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Therefore glorify the name of your Lord, the Great.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: So glorify the name of your Lord, the Supreme.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Then celebrate with praises the name of thy Lord, the Supreme!

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Fasabbih biismi rabbika alAAatheemi

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: So I call the placement of the stars to witness --

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: So I swear by the setting places of the stars.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: No! I swear by the fallings of the stars

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: I swear by the setting of the stars -

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: So I swear by Mawaqi (setting or the mansions, etc.) of the stars (they traverse).

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: I swear by the locations of the stars.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: No! I swear by the positions of the stars –

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Fala! I swear by the Mawaqi` of the stars.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Nay, I swear by the places of the stars -

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: I swear by the places where the stars set!

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: I swear by the fallings of the stars

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Then I swear by the setting of the stars,

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: I do not need to swear by the setting of the stars

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: But nay! I swear by the falling of stars;

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Nay, I swear by the setting of the stars --

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Furthermore I call to witness the setting of the Stars,-

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Fala oqsimu bimawaqiAAi alnnujoomi

translations.author: Qarai

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: and that is a mighty oath, if you but knew

translations.text: And that is indeed a mighty adjuration if ye but knew,-

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Do you, then, take this discourse in light esteem,

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Is it such a talk that you are Mudhinun

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Is it this Statement that ye scorn,

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: What! Do you take lightly this discourse?

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: What, do you hold this discourse in disdain,

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Then is it to this statement that you are indifferent

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Would you say that this statement is a lie?

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Do you then hold this announcement in contempt?

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: How can you regard this discourse with disdain?

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Is it such a Message that ye would hold in light esteem?

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Afabihatha alhadeethi antum mudhinoona

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: You live by calling it a lie.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And you make its denial your share?

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: and do you make it your living to cry lies?

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And make it your provision that ye should belie it?

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And instead (of thanking Allah) for the provision He gives you, on the contrary, you deny Him (by disbelief)!

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: And you make it your livelihood to deny it?

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: and your portion in it is simply that you denounce it as false?

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And you make your provision your denial!

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And make denial thereof your livelihood?

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: And make your denial of it your vocation?

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: do you make it your provision to belie it?

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And make [the thanks for] your provision that you deny [the Provider]?

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Would you reject the sustenance that you receive rather than give thanks?

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And to give (it) the lie you make your means of subsistence.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Do you make its denial your means of livelihood?

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: And have ye made it your livelihood that ye should declare it false?

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: WatajAAaloona rizqakum annakum tukaththiboona

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Then how is it that when the (dying) breath is withdrawn into the throat (and rattles),

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: So why was it not, when the soul reaches up to the throat, –

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Why, but when the soul leaps to the throat of the dying

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Wherefore then - when the soul cometh up to the wind-pipe -

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Then why do you not (intervene) when (the soul of a dying person) reaches the throat?

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: So when it has reached the throat.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Why, then, when the soul leaps up to the throat,

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Then why do you not (intervene) when it reaches Al-Hulqum

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Why, then, when (the soul) cometh up to the throat (of the dying)

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: So when it reaches the throat [of the dying person],

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Why, then, when the soul leaps up to the throat of the dying

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Then why, when the soul at death reaches the throat

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Why can you not help a soul dying

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Why is it not then that when it (soul) comes up to the throat,

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Why, then, when the soul of the dying man reaches the throat,

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Then why do ye not (intervene) when (the soul of the dying man) reaches the throat,-

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Falawla itha balaghati alhulqooma

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: And you wait for the moment (of death),

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Whereas you watch at that moment!

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: and that hour you are watching

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And ye are then looking on.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And you at the moment are looking on,

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: As you are looking on.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: the while you are helplessly watching that he is on the verge of death,

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And you at the moment are looking,

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And ye are at that moment looking

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: and at that moment you are looking on [at his bedside]

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: and you are watching at that time

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And you are at that time looking on -

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: right before your very eyes?

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And you at that time look on--

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: and you are [at that moment] looking on [helplessly] --

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: And ye the while (sit) looking on,-

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Waantum heenaithin tanthuroona

85.arabic: وَنَحْنُ أَقْرَبُ إِلَيْهِ مِنكُمْ وَلَـٰكِن لَّا تُبْصِرُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: We are closer to him than you, even though you cannot see?

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And We are nearer to him than you are, but you cannot see.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: (And We are nigher him than you, but you do not see Us)

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And We are nigher Unto him than ye are, but ye behold not-

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: But We (i.e. Our angels who take the soul) are nearer to him than you, but you see not, [Tafsir At-Tabari, Vol. 27, Page 209]

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: We are nearer to it than you are, but you do not see.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: – at that moment when We are closer to him than you, although you do not see (Us)

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: But We are nearer to him than you, but you see not,

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: - And We are nearer unto him than ye are, but ye see not -

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: —and We are nearer to him than you are, though you do not perceive—

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: We are nearer to him than you, but you do not see

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And Our angels are nearer to him than you, but you do not see -

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: We are closer to him than you, but you cannot see.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And We are nearer to it than you, but you do not see--

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: and We are nearer to him than you, although you cannot see Us --

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: But We are nearer to him than ye, and yet see not,-

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Wanahnu aqrabu ilayhi minkum walakin la tubsiroona

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Then why, if you are not indebted (to Us for life),

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: So why is it not, that if you are not to be repaid, –

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: why, if you are not at Our disposal,

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Wherefore then, if ye are not to be requited

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Then why do you not, if you are exempt from the reckoning and recompense (punishment, etc.)

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: If you are not held to account.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: if you are not subject to anyone's authority,

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Then why do you not -- if you are not Madinin

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Why then, if ye are not in bondage (unto Us),

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: then why do you not restore it, if you are not subject

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: why then, if you are not revived,

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Then why do you not, if you are not to be recompensed,

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: If you are true to your claim that there is no Day of Judgment,

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Then why is it not-- if you are not held under authority--

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: why, then, if you are not subject to Our command,

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Then why do ye not,- If you are exempt from (future) account,-

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Falawla in kuntum ghayra madeeneena

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Can you not bring him back? (Answer) if you are truthful

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: That you bring it back, if you are truthful?

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: do you not bring back his soul, if you speak truly?

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Cause ye it not to return, if ye say sooth?

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Bring back the soul (to its body), if you are truthful?

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Then bring it back, if you are truthful.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: why are you then not able to bring them back to life if you are truthful?

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Return the soul, if you are truthful

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Do ye not force it back, if ye are truthful?

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: [to Divine dispensation], if you are truthful?

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: do you not restore his soul, if you are truthful?

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Bring it back, if you should be truthful?

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: why can you not bring it (the soul) back (to life)?

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: That you send it (not) back-- if you are truthful?

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: do you not cause the soul to return to him if you are truthful in your claim?

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Call back the soul, if ye are true (in the claim of independence)?

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: TarjiAAoonaha in kuntum sadiqeena

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Then, if he is one of the honoured,

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Then if the dying one is of those having proximity, –

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Then, if he be of those brought nigh the Throne,

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Then if he be of the broughtnigh.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Then, if he (the dying person) be of the Muqarrabun (those brought near to Allah),

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: But if he is one of those brought near.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: So if he is one of those who are near-stationed (to Allah),

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Then, if he be of the Muqarrabin,

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Thus if he is of those brought nigh,

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Then, if he be of those brought near,

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: If he is among the near

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And if the deceased was of those brought near to Allah,

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: (If a dying soul) is of those near to God,

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Then if he is one of those drawn nigh (to Allah),

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: But if he [the dying person] is one of those brought near to God,

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Thus, then, if he be of those Nearest to Allah,

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Faamma in kana mina almuqarrabeena

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: There will be peace and plenty, and gardens of tranquility for him.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Then is relief, and flowers – and Gardens of peace.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: there shall be repose and ease, and a Garden of Delight;

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: For him shall be comfort, and fragrance and a Garden of Delight.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: (There is for him) rest and provision, and a Garden of delights (Paradise).

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Then happiness, and flowers, and Garden of Delights.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: then happiness and delight and Gardens of Bliss are his.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Then Rawh, Rayhan and a Garden of Delights.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Then breath of life, and plenty, and a Garden of delight.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: then ease, abundance, and a garden of bliss.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: there shall be calmness and ease, a Garden of Delight.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Then [for him is] rest and bounty and a garden of pleasure.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: it will have rest, happiness, and a beautiful Paradise.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Then happiness and bounty and a garden of bliss.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: then for him there shall be comfort and plenty and a Garden of Bliss;

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: (There is for him) Rest and Satisfaction, and a Garden of Delights.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Farawhun warayhanun wajannatu naAAeemin

90.arabic: وَأَمَّآ إِن كَانَ مِنْ أَصْحَـٰبِ ٱلْيَمِينِ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: If he is of those of the right hand,

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And if he is of those on the right, –

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: and if he be a Companion of the Right:

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And if he be of the fellows on the right hand,

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And if he (the dying person) be of those on the Right Hand,

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: And if he is one of those on the Right.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: And if he is one of the People on the Right,

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And if he be of those on the right,

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And if he is of those on the right hand,

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: And if he be of the People of the Right Hand,

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: If he is a Companion of the Right

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And if he was of the companions of the right,

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: If it is of the people of the right hand,

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And if he is one of those on the right hand,

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: and if he is of those who are on the Right,

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: And if he be of the Companions of the Right Hand,

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Waamma in kana min ashabi alyameeni

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: There will be the salutation by those of the right hand: "Peace on you.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Then upon you is the greetings of peace (O dear Prophet Mohammed – peace and blessings be upon him), from those on the right.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: 'Peace be upon thee, Companion of the Right!'

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Then: peace Unto thee, for thou art of those on the right hand.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Then there is safety and peace (from the Punishment of Allah) for (you as you are from) those on the Right Hand.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Then, “Peace upon you,” from those on the Right.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: he will be welcomed by the words: “Peace to you” from the People on the Right.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Then Salam (peace) to you from those on the right.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Then (the greeting) "Peace be unto thee" from those on the right hand.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: then [he will be told,] ‘Peace be on you, from the People of the Right Hand!’

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: (he will be greeted with): 'Peace be upon you, Companion of the Right'

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Then [the angels will say], "Peace for you; [you are] from the companions of the right."

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: it will be with the people of the right hand, living in peace and security.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Then peace to you from those on the right hand.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: he will be greeted with, "Peace be to you," by those on the Right.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: (For him is the salutation), "Peace be unto thee", from the Companions of the Right Hand.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Fasalamun laka min ashabi alyameeni

92.arabic: وَأَمَّآ إِن كَانَ مِنَ ٱلْمُكَذِّبِينَ ٱلضَّآلِّينَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: But if he is of the deniers and the errants,

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And if he is from the deniers, the astray, –

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: But if he be of them that cried lies, and went astray,

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And if he be of the beliers, the erring,

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: But if he (the dying person) be of the denying (of the Resurrection), the erring (away from the Right Path of Islamic Monotheism),

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: But if he is one of the deniers, the mistaken.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: And if he is one of those who give the lie (to this Message) and go astray,

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: But if he be of the denying, the erring,

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: But if he is of the rejecters, the erring,

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: But if he be of the impugners, the astray ones,

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: But, if he is of those who belied, and went astray,

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: But if he was of the deniers [who were] astray,

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: If it is of those who have rejected the Truth and have gone astray,

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And if he is one of the rejecters, the erring ones,

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: But if he is one of those who rejected [the truth] and went astray,

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: And if he be of those who treat (Truth) as Falsehood, who go wrong,

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Waamma in kana mina almukaththibeena alddalleena

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: The welcome will be boiling water

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Then his reception is the hot boiling water.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: there shall be a hospitality of boiling water

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Then an entertainment of boiling water.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Then for him is entertainment with boiling water.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Then a welcome of Inferno.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: then he will be served boiling water,

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Then for him is an entertainment with Hamim.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Then the welcome will be boiling water

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: then a treat of boiling water

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: there shall be a hospitality of boiling water,

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Then [for him is] accommodation of scalding water

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: its dwelling will be boiling water

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: He shall have an entertainment of boiling water,

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: he will be welcomed with boiling water.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: For him is Entertainment with Boiling Water.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Fanuzulun min hameemin

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: And the roasting in Hell.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And a hurling into the blazing fire.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: and the roasting in Hell.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And roasting in a Blaze.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And burning in Hell-fire.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: And burning in Hell.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: and will be scorched by the Fire.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And entry in Hellfire.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And roasting at hell-fire.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: and entry into hell.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: and a roasting in Hell.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And burning in Hellfire

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: and the heat of hell fire.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And burning in hell.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: He will burn in Hell.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: And burning in Hell-Fire.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Watasliyatu jaheemin

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: This is indeed the ultimate truth.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: This is indeed an utmost certainty.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Surely this is the truth of certainty.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Verily this! this is the very truth.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Verily, this! This is an absolute Truth with certainty.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: This is the certain truth.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: That indeed is the absolute truth.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Verily, this! This is an absolute truth with certainty.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Lo! this is certain truth.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Indeed this is certain truth.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Indeed, this is the certain truth.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Indeed, this is the true certainty,

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: This is the absolute Truth and certainty.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Most surely this is a certain truth.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: This is indeed the indubitable truth.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Verily, this is the Very Truth and Certainly.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Inna hatha lahuwa haqqu alyaqeeni

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Then praise your Lord, the most supreme.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Therefore (O dear Prophet Mohammed – peace and blessings be upon him) proclaim the Purity of the name of your Lord, the Greatest.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Then magnify the Name of thy Lord, the All-mighty.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Wherefore hallow thou the name of thy Lord, the Mighty!

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: So glorify with praises the Name of your Lord, the Most Great.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: So glorify the Name of your Lord, the Magnificent

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: So glorify the name of your Great Lord.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: So, glorify with praises the Name of your Lord, the Most Great.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Therefor (O Muhammad) praise the name of thy Lord, the Tremendous.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: So celebrate the Name of your Lord, the All-supreme!

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: So exalt the Name of your Lord, the Great.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: So exalt the name of your Lord, the Most Great.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: So glorify the name of your Lord, the Great One.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Therefore glorify the name of your Lord, the Great.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: So glorify the name of your Lord, the Supreme.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: So celebrate with praises the name of thy Lord, the Supreme.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Fasabbih biismi rabbika alAAatheemi
